working_on: Routine-protection refinement of S-0089's MCP-availability tightening. User clarification: MCP unavailability is easy to fix (Claude Desktop reboot); user just needs visibility. Routine sessions running overnight / AFK must NOT be killed by intermittent MCP. S-0089's `mempalace_diary_write_skipped_invalid_token` hard-fail blocks the close when substrate is alive but token is present — exactly the routine-killing scenario the user pushed back against. Convert the invalid-token path from hard-fail to soft-warn with mode-aware body (engine LOUD ⚠️ surface; routine standard one-line). Visibility still preserved via LOUD body for engine + standard for routine; routines close cleanly. Campaign plan reorder: Issue #41 (C3) shifts S-0090 → S-0091.
outcome_summary: Converted S-0089's `mempalace_diary_write_skipped_invalid_token` HARD-FAIL to `mempalace_diary_write_skipped_substrate_intermittent` SOFT-WARN with mode-aware body. Engine: LOUD ⚠️ MCP INTERMITTENT — DO NOT BURY THIS. Routine: standard one-line. Routines running overnight / AFK no longer killed by intermittent MCP per user directive. Visibility preserved via LOUD body for engine + soft-warn for routine archive review. The S-0087/S-0088 burial pattern remains hard to repeat — LOUD body surfaces the substrate-alive + token-present contradiction explicitly. ADR 0056 amended in-place (no supersession); 2 new pytests replace the prior invalid-token test (29 total in test_validate_mempalace_adoption.py); mypy --strict + ruff clean. Validator at shutdown: 0 hard-fails. Campaign plan reorder: Issue #41 (C3) shifts S-0090 → S-0091.

scope_delivery.explanation: User clarified at S-0089 close that MCP unavailability is easy to fix via Claude Desktop reboot; visibility is the goal, NOT close-blocking. S-0089's invalid-token hard-fail killed routines on intermittent MCP — exactly the scenario the user ruled out. S-0090 converts the path to soft-warn with mode-aware body. Engine sessions get LOUD ⚠️ surface preserving visibility; routine sessions get standard one-line so they close cleanly with the warn surfaced in archive review. The S-0087/S-0088 burial pattern remains structurally hard to repeat. 2 test replacements covering engine LOUD body + routine standard body. ADR 0056 amended in-place. No mid-session descope or reorder.
